description:
A developer has dropped four loose files into the project root directory

        /home/user/dev_project

    The files currently present in the directory are:

        /home/user/dev_project/app.py
        /home/user/dev_project/test_app.py
        /home/user/dev_project/README.txt
        /home/user/dev_project/changelog.md

    Your job is to reorganise the project so it matches the layout that the build-scripts expect.

    Required end-state of the file-tree
    -----------------------------------
    • /home/user/dev_project/src/app.py
    • /home/user/dev_project/tests/test_app.py
    • /home/user/dev_project/docs/README.txt
    • /home/user/dev_project/docs/changelog.md
    • /home/user/dev_project/FILE_MANIFEST.txt      ← a new text file you must create

    Detailed requirements
    ---------------------
    1. Create three **new** sub-directories directly under /home/user/dev_project
    
           src      (code lives here)
           docs     (documentation lives here)
           tests    (test-suites live here)

    2. Move each of the existing files into its appropriate directory as shown in the
       “Required end-state” section above.  No copies should remain in the project root.

    3. Produce a plain-text manifest called FILE_MANIFEST.txt in the project root.
       The manifest must list every project file (but NOT directories), one per line,
       using paths that are **relative to /home/user/dev_project**.

       The lines must be sorted in strict ASCII lexicographic order, **no extra blank
       lines**, and end with a single UNIX newline (LF).  The exact target contents are:

           docs/README.txt
           docs/changelog.md
           src/app.py
           tests/test_app.py

       The automated test will:
         • verify that the four lines above appear in that precise order,
         • verify there is exactly one newline character after the final line,
         • confirm that each listed file actually exists at the stated location.

    4. Do NOT delete or rename any of the four files.  Only move them and create the required
       directories and manifest file.

    5. You do not need root privileges; everything must stay inside /home/user/dev_project.

    Hints
    -----
    • Typical commands you might consider: mkdir, mv, ls, sort, printf or echo with redirection.
    • Remember the manifest must use **relative** paths, not absolute paths.
    • Double-check the final newline requirement: exactly one trailing LF, nothing more, nothing less.

    When you are finished, the directory tree and manifest should match the specification above.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth state that the automated grader will expect
    after the agent finishes the task.
    -->

    <project_root>/home/user/dev_project</project_root>

    <!-- Files that exist before the task begins -->
    <initial_files>
        <file>/home/user/dev_project/app.py</file>
        <file>/home/user/dev_project/test_app.py</file>
        <file>/home/user/dev_project/README.txt</file>
        <file>/home/user/dev_project/changelog.md</file>
    </initial_files>

    <!-- Directories that must exist at the end -->
    <required_directories>
        <dir>/home/user/dev_project/src</dir>
        <dir>/home/user/dev_project/docs</dir>
        <dir>/home/user/dev_project/tests</dir>
    </required_directories>

    <!-- Final absolute locations of the four project files -->
    <final_files>
        <file>/home/user/dev_project/src/app.py</file>
        <file>/home/user/dev_project/tests/test_app.py</file>
        <file>/home/user/dev_project/docs/README.txt</file>
        <file>/home/user/dev_project/docs/changelog.md</file>
    </final_files>

    <!-- Manifest file path and its exact required content -->
    <manifest>
        <path>/home/user/dev_project/FILE_MANIFEST.txt</path>
        <content>
docs/README.txt
docs/changelog.md
src/app.py
tests/test_app.py
</content>
    </manifest>
